title: rubus hirtus var. adenoneurus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. adenoneurus, commonly known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a rare and lesser-known species of the Rubus genus, characterized by its unique combination of physical and morphological characteristics. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. adenoneurus are thought to include the reduction of oxidative stress, improvement of cardiovascular health, and potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, often used to support digestive health and immune function.
